summary: The Lampe laboratory performs studies to find blood tests or protein biomarkers that can indicate if and where a person has cancer and if it is present, how to best treat it. Specifically, they are working to find early detection, recurrence or response biomarkers of colon, breast, and lung cancer. Once the biomarker is defined, they make drugs based on that biomarker so the tumors can be directly attacked.

organization: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center - Mark Roth
locations: On Site: 1100 Fairview Ave N, Seattle, WA 98109, Roth Lab
summary: I study the phenomenon of suspended animation in the nematode C. When deprived of oxygen, adult nematodes can survive in an alternative death-like state for anywhere from 48 to 72 hours at room temperature. This phenomenon, termed suspended animation, can be replicated in other animals - including humans – and can prove useful in industry.
activities: The Roth lab is unique in how it conducts science. Rather than searching for genotype- phenotype correlations, my mentor finds it more convenient to assay for life and death directly.

searchText: fred hutchinson cancer center - mark roth fred hutchinson cancer center mark roth biomedical / health research research: bio/medical on site: 1100 fairview ave n, seattle, wa 98109, roth lab seattle 2026 mark roth mroth@fredhutch.org evan wang research: bio/medical i study the phenomenon of suspended animation in the nematode c. when deprived of oxygen, adult nematodes can survive in an alternative death-like state for anywhere from 48 to 72 hours at room temperature. this phenomenon, termed suspended animation, can be replicated in other animals - including humans – and can prove useful in industry. the roth lab is unique in how it conducts science. rather than searching for genotype- phenotype correlations, my mentor finds it more convenient to assay for life and death directly. official fred hutch pages describe a seattle cancer research and care center with a long research history, global reach, thousands of employees, and nobel prize-winning scientific work.

organization: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center - Natalie Duerkopp
summary: The Greenberg Lab is an immunology lab focused on the research of various cell markers of primarily CD4 and CD8 T-cells in combatting cancer cells. Various processes including flow cytometry, and DNA isolation and extraction are used to target and identify the cells of interest, allowing for cell modification and design to be tested on mice with cancerous tumors.
activities: Cell passaging - Learning flow cytometry - Cell counting - Lab chores - Gel electrophoresis - Making agar plates - Bacterial transformation - Overnight cell cultures - Mice spleen processing

organization: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center - Roland K. Strong
summary: The Strong lab at Fred Hutch mainly focuses on using protein crystallography to study the functions and binding properties of the immunoprotein siderocalin. During my time here, I learned to do protein sequencing, molecular cloning, and protein crystallization, as well as extract protein crystals for X-Ray diffraction. I also got to learn basic lab procedures like purifying DNA, performing size-exclusion chromatography, and setting up crystallization plates.
activities: Design and make a protein through protein sequencing and molecular cloning - Learned protein crystallization and used crystal structures to study the function and binding properties of proteins

organization: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center - Dr. Jean De Dieu Tapsoba
summary: Fred Hutch is a research institute that conducts medical research in many different areas. Although the primary focus is cancer, I worked as an assistant to Dr. Tapsoba, using R to analyze and display data gathered from HIV/AIDS prevention research in East Africa. The goal of the project is to better understand demographic factors that result in HIV/AIDS transmission and the ways in which effective treatment can be applied.
activities: - Experience using the R statistical programming language to analyze, model, and display data - Experience with various R libraries like dplyr, ggplot2, reshape2, and lubridate - Experience working with datasets from real studies

organization: Fred Hutchinson Research Center - Siegen McKellar / Andrea Belleville
summary: Investigated the role of the organelle vault in cancer cells in an MVP-KO mouse model, seeking to inform the development of better cancer therapies. Performed PCR, gel electrophoresis, nucleic acid purifications, DNA sequencing, and other standard in vitro experimental approaches.
activities: It's interesting how we know so little about so a ubiquitous organelle. I was mentored by two people in the UW's prestigious MD/PhO program. I got to do e lot of lab techniques I learned in the past (e.g., PCR, gel electrophoresis) in e real lab setting. The lab I work at is very interdisciplinary. So, during lab meetings, I got to learn about everything from poison exons to synthetic introns and vaults.

summary: Fairhall Laboratory is a primarily computational neuroscience lab that studies Hydra, a microorganism with simple behaviors. Most of my time working in the lab was spent doing video analysis using software such as Python, Matlab, ImageJ, and Icy. I worked on a variety of different videos from calcium wave videos to 30- minute full body behavioral videos. Furthermore, it was a great way to learn about neuroscience and how research works in the real world.
activities: - Learning how to use computer science to process behavior in different microscope videos. - Weekly lab meetings featuring in depth discussion and planning for future research. - Drafting research papers and presentations and practicing scientific communication.

summary: ADCS is a dental practice in downtown Seattle that sees patients four days a week. Recently, they decided to switch dental software from a software called DSN to a new software called Dentrix. As such, they needed help transferring patient data from DSN to Dentrix.
